title: IdeaPlan brainstorming state
description: Unified IdeaPlan document shape and agent contract when status is brainstorming. The brainstorm section is required; the plan section must be absent. Session records are mutable in place: start-brainstorm-session creates the slot; update-brainstorm-session fills inputs and scores progressively until completedAt is set. Session completion is not lifecycle completion: agents stop after updating the session unless the operator explicitly says brainstorming is finished and asks to start planning.

x-sessionMutability.description: start-brainstorm-session allocates a sessionId and timestamps; update-brainstorm-session progressively fills inputs, computes scores, and may set completedAt without replacing the session row. Setting completedAt finishes the guided session only; complete-brainstorm is reserved for explicit operator confirmation that brainstorming as a whole is finished.

questions.prompt: What feature ideas or solution directions should we capture for this idea?
questions.guidance: Contribute proposals yourself; do not only ask the operator. Persist with update-brainstorm-session ideation.featureIdeas as {text,rationale?} items.

questions.prompt: What perspectives, constraints, or framing should shape this idea?
questions.guidance: Offer at least one perspective the operator may not have stated. Persist with update-brainstorm-session ideation.perspectives.

questions.prompt: What should success look like if this idea ships?
questions.guidance: Capture operator-visible outcomes and expectations. Persist with update-brainstorm-session ideation.expectations.

questions.prompt: Save brainstorm progress or finish and move toward planning?
questions.guidance: After each ideation turn, offer save-and-return (update-brainstorm-session without completedAt) vs finish-session (set completedAt when the operator confirms). Run complete-brainstorm only when the operator explicitly says brainstorming is finished.

questions.prompt: In one or two sentences, what problem or opportunity does this idea address?
questions.guidance: Restate the operator's idea in plain language before scoring. Capture the core pain or opportunity, not implementation details.

questions.prompt: Who benefits first if this idea succeeds?
questions.guidance: Name the primary user or operator persona. Secondary audiences can be noted in session notes later.

questions.prompt: How strong is the expected impact if this idea succeeds (1=minimal, 10=transformative)?
questions.guidance: Score the magnitude of outcome improvement, not confidence that it will happen.

questions.prompt: How many users, workflows, or systems would materially benefit (1=narrow, 10=broad)?
questions.guidance: Count durable beneficiaries across the workspace, not one-off convenience.

questions.prompt: How time-sensitive is pursuing this idea now (1=can wait, 10=blocking now)?
questions.guidance: Higher scores mean delay has clear cost or risk; lower scores mean the idea is valuable but not urgent.

questions.prompt: How well does this idea align with current roadmap and strategic goals (1=orthogonal, 10=core)?
questions.guidance: Score alignment with active phase goals and maintainer priorities, not generic importance.

questions.prompt: How high is the technical execution risk (1=well understood, 10=high unknowns)?
questions.guidance: Consider architecture novelty, dependency risk, and verification difficulty.

questions.prompt: How high is the operational or rollout risk (1=low blast radius, 10=wide blast radius)?
questions.guidance: Include support burden, migration pain, and failure impact on active workflows.

questions.prompt: How many unresolved unknowns remain (1=few, 10=many critical gaps)?
questions.guidance: Score open questions that could invalidate the approach if left unanswered.

questions.prompt: If this idea fails or must roll back, how hard is recovery (1=easy rollback, 10=hard to unwind)?
questions.guidance: Higher scores mean partial delivery or bad assumptions would be costly to reverse.

questions.prompt: What T-shirt size best fits the first shippable slice?
questions.guidance: Estimate relative delivery size for a focused implementation slice, not the entire multi-phase vision.

questions.prompt: How complex is the work independent of calendar time (1=straightforward, 10=deeply complex)?
questions.guidance: T-shirt size and complexity are weighted independently in effortScore — a high-complexity M item intentionally scores higher than a low-complexity L item because complexity carries 60% of the effort blend.

questions.prompt: How strong is the supporting evidence (1=anecdotal, 10=strong proof)?
questions.guidance: Include prior art, operator feedback, telemetry, or prior experiments.

questions.prompt: How available is the expertise needed to execute (1=scarce, 10=ready now)?
questions.guidance: Consider team familiarity with affected modules and required review depth.

questions.prompt: How clear is the problem and solution boundary (1=vague, 10=crisp)?
questions.guidance: Higher clarity means acceptance criteria and scope boundaries are already obvious.

questions.prompt: List the top unknowns that could change the scores or scope.
questions.guidance: Prefer concrete questions over generic risk statements. Unknowns here inform planning, not session scoring.

questions.prompt: What alternatives did you consider and why is this approach preferred?
questions.guidance: Capture at least one credible alternative, including doing nothing, and why it was deprioritized.

questions.prompt: Add any session notes, assumptions, or follow-ups for the planning phase.
questions.guidance: Keep notes concise and actionable. Do not duplicate computed scores here. After setting completedAt with update-brainstorm-session, stop and summarize the session; do not transition to planning unless the operator explicitly confirms brainstorming is finished.

computeSteps.formula: effortScore = (tShirtSizeScore × 0.40) + (complexity × 0.60); tShirtSizeScore map: XS=2, S=4, M=6, L=8, XL=10
computeSteps.description: Blend of T-shirt size and complexity. High-complexity M intentionally scores higher than low-complexity L.
Priority blends upside and confidence minus risk and effort, scaled to 0–100 via rawPriority normalization.

synthesisStep.formula: if sessions.length===1 then synthesized=session.scores else for each score field synthesized[field]=latest[field]×0.60+mean(prior[field])×0.40
synthesisStep.description: N=1 edge case: when sessions.length===1, synthesized=session scores; else synthesized=latest×0.60+mean(prior)×0.40.
